prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "哥本哈根侧平板" (Copenhagen plank (bent-leg variant)) exercise, side view from the open side. Person posture: Athletic person holding a Copenhagen side plank (long-lever variant with bent bottom leg) on the LEFT side: lying on the LEFT side. Left forearm flat on the ground (left elbow directly under left shoulder, left forearm perpendicular to the body line). The TOP (RIGHT) LEG is FULLY EXTENDED STRAIGHT forward with the right ANKLE / LOWER SHIN resting on top of a knee-height bench/box (the bench supports the body weight at the foot end of the top leg). The BOTTOM (LEFT) LEG is BENT at the knee at about 90 degrees, with the left foot tucked back behind the buttocks and the left knee pointing forward (the bent left leg is held IN THE AIR, OFF THE GROUND, not touching anything). Hips lifted high off the ground so the body forms a roughly straight line from the LEFT shoulder through the hips to the right foot supported on the bench. Right arm extended straight up toward the ceiling. Plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. CRITICAL: TOP (RIGHT) leg is STRAIGHT and rests on the bench at the foot/ankle; BOTTOM (LEFT) leg is BENT at the knee and held in the air. The bottom leg is NOT straight, the top leg is NOT bent.. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: adductor muscles of the bottom (left) leg (inner thigh). Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: obliques and gluteus medius and shoulder stabilizers. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "内收肌群" pointing to the adductor muscles (inner thigh); "腹斜肌" pointing to the obliques; "臀中肌 / 肩稳定肌" pointing to the gluteus medius and shoulder stabilizers. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
